papers.title_en: SafeGen: LLM-Driven Assertion Generation and Fault Criticality Evaluation for Functional Safety
papers.authors: Xuanyi Tan, Arjun Chaudhuri, Rubin Parekhji, Krishnendu Chakrabarty
papers.evidence: LLM驱动的硬件功能安全断言生成
papers.tldr: 自动驾驶和电动汽车对芯片功能安全提出高要求，传统仿真故障分析过于保守且缺乏语义解释。本文提出SafeGen框架，利用大语言模型和超知识图谱提取规范并生成功能安全断言，结合形式验证和门级到RTL故障映射实现故障关键性语义分级。在FOC系统上的实验表明，SafeGen生成的断言质量优于现有方法，故障评估语义可解释性更强。该工作为功能安全评估提供了可追溯、高精度的自动化方案。
papers.abstract_en: With advances in autonomous driving and electric vehicle technologies, functional safety has become a critical requirement in automotive chip design. Traditional simulation-based fault analysis is often overly conservative at the module level and fails to accurately reflect fault criticality. This paper presents SafeGen, an LLM-driven, formal-verification-assisted framework for functional-safety-oriented fault criticality assessment. SafeGen leverages large language models (LLMs) and a document-level Hyper Knowledge Graph (HyperKG) that incorporates Failure Modes, Effects, and Diagnostic Analysis (FMEDA) guidelines to extract verifiable specifications from design and safety documents and evaluate their relevance to overall system safety. The HyperKG is further enriched with register-transfer-level (RTL) information to guide the generation of Functional Safety Assertions (FSAs) that are both semantically grounded and design-aware. Each assertion is linked to its corresponding specification, enabling traceable reasoning throughout the assessment process. A gate-to-RTL fault-mapping mechanism supporting both stuck-at and bridging faults, combined with formal property verification (FPV), enables semantic-level fault criticality grading based on specification-linked assertion violations. A digital-physical co-simulation platform for a field-oriented control (FOC) system is developed to validate SafeGen. Experimental results demonstrate that SafeGen generates higher-quality assertions than existing LLM-based assertion generation frameworks while providing greater semantic interpretability in fault criticality assessment compared with traditional simulation-based approaches.

papers.title_en: Evidence-Driven LLM Agent for C-to-Synthesizable-C Conversion and Verification
papers.authors: Zhe Zhao, Hongbing Lang, Zhihan Xiao, Luke Ztz Hu, John Imoleayo Adebisi, Songping Mai
papers.evidence: LLM智能体用于C到可综合C的转换及HLS流程中的验证
papers.tldr: HLS工具链仅接受可综合C子集，但现有LLM修复系统只覆盖早期阶段且直接使用原始日志导致修复脆弱。本文提出闭环生成-验证-诊断-修复智能体工作流，包含渐进式错配定位链（PMLC）和类型查询两阶段证据RAG。实验表明该方法显著优于现有模型，实现了C到可综合C的高效转换与可靠验证。
papers.abstract_en: Software-compilable C programs routinely fail to complete the four-stage pipeline of a high-level synthesis (HLS) toolchain -- compilation, C simulation (CSim), synthesis, and C/RTL co-simulation (CoSim) -- because HLS accepts only a synthesizable subset of C (HLS-C). Yet most existing large language model (LLM) systems built for HLS code repair only cover the early pipeline stages and feed raw tool logs directly to the model, yielding brittle and hard-to-reproduce fixes. We formulate C-to-HLS-C conversion as a closed-loop generation-verification-diagnosis-repair problem on an HLS tool (Xilinx Vitis), contributing three components: an end-to-end workflow of cooperating agents closed by the four-stage verifier under strict evidence isolation; a Progressive Mismatch Localization Chain (PMLC) that localizes CSim/CoSim mismatches through log normalization, AST backward slicing, and dual-trace instrumentation; and a typed-query, two-stage evidence RAG backed by a self-evolving, family-routed repair-card pool. Experimental results show that the proposed workflow substantially outperforms all comparable state-of-the-art models.

papers.title_en: On Parameterized Verification Over Tree Topologies
papers.authors: Romain Delpy, Anca Muscholl, Grégoire Sutre
papers.evidence: 适用于硬件系统的参数化验证技术
papers.tldr: 参数化验证在树拓扑上通过两种界限实现可判定。深度界限下安全检测复杂度关联快速增长层级；阶段界限（上下同步交替次数）下，固定阶段数为EXPSPACE完全，阶段数作为输入时为2EXPSPACE完全。这些结果为树状结构系统验证提供了复杂度分类。
papers.abstract_en: Parameterized verification of finite-state processes with rendez-vous synchronization is notoriously undecidable when processes are linearly ordered. In this paper we study two kinds of bounds under which we determine the complexity of safety checking over tree topologies. When bounding the depth we obtain that the complexity is related to the fast growing hierarchy. Our second bound limits the alternations between upwards and downwards synchronizations in the tree (phases), and occurs naturally in many concrete settings. If we fix the number of phases then the complexity of safety checking is EXPSPACE complete, and if the number of phases is part of the input it is 2EXPSPACE complete (both for arbitrary depth).

papers.title_en: Structural Certification for Reliable Physical Design with Language Models
papers.evidence: 语言模型用于物理设计可靠性认证
papers.tldr: 语言模型生成物理设计不可靠，现有认证方法易被伪造。提出PHACT框架，将断言权移出模型，由确定性引擎从固定输入推导认证量，实现不可伪造的认证。在涵盖两模型、两解码温度和故障引擎的80次对抗测试中，零虚假认证。该方法从根本上防止伪造，为可靠物理设计提供保障。
papers.abstract_en: An unreliable language model can be made to produce reliable physical designs if the authority to assert is moved out of the model: the model proposes, and a deterministic engine alone certifies, returning certified, impossible, or unknown. We introduce Physics-Anchored Certification (PHACT), a propose-certify loop spanning five scientific domains, and identify what makes such a certificate trustworthy. A checker that accepts a model-supplied value can be forged; deriving the certified quantity from fixed inputs instead makes forgery impossible by construction. Across eighty adversarial trials spanning two models, two decoding temperatures, and a deliberately faulted engine, this contract produced zero false certifications.

papers.title_en: Modal CEGAR-tableaux with RECAR and resolution-based SAT-shortcuts
papers.evidence: CEGAR-tableaux方法可迁移至硬件形式验证
papers.tldr: 本文针对模态逻辑可满足性判定问题，在CEGAR-tableaux基础上引入SAT短路。探索了两种扩展：使用已有RECAR方法和全新的基于模态归结定理证明器KSP的SAT短路。实验表明，基于KSP的方法在大型可满足问题上显著优于单独使用CEGARBox++或KSP，而RECAR方法不具竞争力。这是首次有效集成SAT、tableaux和resolution方法，性能超过其组成部分。
papers.abstract_en: We investigate two approaches for extending CEGAR-tableaux with SAT-shortcuts using a previously known approach called RECAR but also a totally new approach using the modal resolution theorem prover KSP  as an oracle. Our experiments using our C++ implementation CEGARBox++ of CEGAR-tableaux show that:   (1) CEGARBox++ with RECAR SAT-shortcuts is not competitive   (2) CEGARBox++ using KSP to provide SAT-shortcuts is superior to both CEGARBox++ and KSP,   particularly on large satisfiable problems.   As far as we know, this is the first effective integration of SAT, tableaux and resolution methods  for modal satisfiability which performs better than its parts.
